# contact: info@cadtri.com

# CADTRI Drafting & Permit Services

> Permit-ready architectural drawings for Florida, Texas, and North Carolina
> projects. Expert drafting and permit coordination that clears building
> department review.

CADTRI is a professional architectural drafting and permit support company
serving Florida, Texas, and North Carolina. The team produces complete permit
sets, ADU packages, garage conversion drawings, home addition permit sets, and
all construction documentation required by building departments across all
three states. CADTRI serves contractors, architects, developers, and property
owners in Miami, Fort Lauderdale, Tampa, Orlando, Jacksonville, Austin, Dallas,
Houston, San Antonio, Charlotte, Raleigh, Durham, and Asheville.
All services are delivered remotely — permit-ready drawings sent digitally.

Full knowledge base: https://www.cadtri.com/index.md
Contact: info@cadtri.com

---

## Permitting Services

- [Permit Set Preparation](https://www.cadtri.com/services/permit-set-preparation): Complete permit set preparation for Florida, Texas, and North Carolina jurisdictions — drawings coordinated to pass plan check on first submission
- [ADU Permit Packages](https://www.cadtri.com/services/adu-permit-packages): Full ADU permit packages for detached, attached, garage conversion, and JADU projects in Florida, Texas, and North Carolina
- [City Comments Response](https://www.cadtri.com/services/city-comments-response): Responding to plan check correction letters from Florida, Texas, and North Carolina building departments with revised drawings and organized response letters
- [Title 24 Energy Compliance](https://www.cadtri.com/services/title-24-energy-compliance): Title 24 energy compliance documentation — CF1R forms, HERS reports, prescriptive and performance compliance methods
- [Home Addition Packages](https://www.cadtri.com/services/home-addition-packages): Home addition permit packages for Florida, Texas, and North Carolina residential projects
- [Garage Conversion Packages](https://www.cadtri.com/services/garage-conversion-packages): Garage conversion ADU permit packages — habitable space conversion drawings
- [Tenant Improvement Packages](https://www.cadtri.com/services/tenant-improvement-packages): Commercial tenant improvement permit sets for Florida, Texas, and North Carolina jurisdictions
- [Solar and EV Permit Packages](https://www.cadtri.com/services/solar-ev-permit-packages): Solar panel and EV charger permit packages
- [Pool and Spa Permit Packages](https://www.cadtri.com/services/pool-spa-permits): Pool and spa permit drawing packages
- [Short-Term Rental Conversion Permits](https://www.cadtri.com/services/short-term-rental-permits): Short-term rental conversion permit documentation
- [Accessory Structure Permits](https://www.cadtri.com/services/accessory-structure-permits): Permit packages for sheds, carports, and other accessory structures
- [Interior Remodel Packages](https://www.cadtri.com/services/interior-remodel-packages): Interior remodel permit sets for residential and commercial projects
- [Historic District Submissions](https://www.cadtri.com/services/historic-district-submissions): Permit coordination for projects within Florida, Texas, and North Carolina historic district overlays
- [Deferred Submittal Packages](https://www.cadtri.com/services/deferred-submittal-packages): Deferred submittal documentation for post-permit specialty systems

---

## Drawing Services

- [Architectural Drafting](https://www.cadtri.com/services/architectural-drafting): Professional architectural CAD drafting for residential and commercial projects
- [As-Built Documentation](https://www.cadtri.com/services/as-built-documentation): As-built drawings for existing structures requiring permit record updates
- [Contractor Bid Package](https://www.cadtri.com/services/contractor-bid-package): Construction document packages formatted for contractor bidding
- [Interior Detail Package](https://www.cadtri.com/services/interior-detail-package): Detailed interior construction drawings for permit and construction use
- [Site Plan Package](https://www.cadtri.com/services/site-plan-package): Site plans showing lot coverage, setbacks, utilities, and zoning compliance
- [Record Drawing Updates](https://www.cadtri.com/services/record-drawing-updates): Updating permit record drawings to reflect constructed conditions
- [Demolition Permit Drawings](https://www.cadtri.com/services/demolition-permit-drawings): Demolition permit drawing sets for Florida, Texas, and North Carolina jurisdictions
- [Redline to CAD Conversion](https://www.cadtri.com/services/redline-to-cad): Converting hand-marked redlines into permit-ready CAD drawings
- [Fire and Life Safety Drawings](https://www.cadtri.com/services/fire-life-safety-drawings): Fire and life safety drawing packages for permit review
- [Signage Permit Drawings](https://www.cadtri.com/services/signage-permit-drawings): Signage permit drawing sets for Florida, Texas, and North Carolina jurisdictions

---

## Strategy and Coordination Services

- [Project Strategy](https://www.cadtri.com/services/project-strategy): Project strategy consultation — scope definition, permit pathway, documentation planning
- [Feasibility Study](https://www.cadtri.com/services/feasibility-study): Pre-project feasibility analysis — zoning, setbacks, FAR, lot coverage, code constraints
- [Zoning Code Research](https://www.cadtri.com/services/zoning-code-research): Zoning code research for Florida, Texas, and North Carolina jurisdictions
- [Permit Pathway Analysis](https://www.cadtri.com/services/permit-pathway-analysis): Permit pathway analysis — ministerial vs. discretionary, SB 9, ADU law, CEQA
- [Pre-Purchase Assessment](https://www.cadtri.com/services/pre-purchase-assessment): Pre-purchase property assessment for development potential and permit feasibility
- [Scope Definition](https://www.cadtri.com/services/scope-definition): Defining project scope before design and permit documentation begins
- [Design Options Study](https://www.cadtri.com/services/design-options-study): Evaluating design alternatives against zoning, code, and budget constraints
- [Compliance Gap Analysis](https://www.cadtri.com/services/compliance-gap-analysis): Identifying code and zoning compliance gaps before permit submission
- [Structural Coordination](https://www.cadtri.com/services/structural-coordination): Coordination with structural engineers for permit-ready structural drawings
- [MEP Coordination](https://www.cadtri.com/services/mep-coordination): MEP (mechanical, electrical, plumbing) coordination for permit submissions
- [Entitlement Support](https://www.cadtri.com/services/entitlement-support): Support for discretionary entitlement applications and planning submissions
- [Pre-Application Meeting Prep](https://www.cadtri.com/services/pre-application-meeting-prep): Pre-application meeting preparation and documentation for city review
- [Construction Administration Support](https://www.cadtri.com/services/construction-administration): Construction phase drawing support and RFI coordination
- [Code and Compliance Review](https://www.cadtri.com/services/code-compliance-review): Florida, Texas, and North Carolina building codes and local jurisdiction code compliance review
- [BIM Coordination](https://www.cadtri.com/services/bim-coordination): BIM coordination for complex projects requiring model-based documentation

- [Renderings and Visualization](https://www.cadtri.com/services/renderings-visualization): Architectural renderings for permit, planning, and presentation use
- [Digital Walkthroughs](https://www.cadtri.com/services/digital-walkthroughs): 3D digital walkthrough models for design review and client presentation
- [3D Staging](https://www.cadtri.com/services/3d-staging): 3D virtual staging for real estate and development marketing

## Resource Guides

These articles answer common questions about permits, ADUs, and construction documentation in Florida, Texas, and North Carolina.

- [What Is a Permit Set?](https://www.cadtri.com/resources/what-is-a-permit-set): Complete guide to permit set components — site plan, floor plans, elevations, sections, details, energy compliance
- [ADU Permit in Florida, Texas, and North Carolina](https://www.cadtri.com/resources/how-to-get-adu-permit-in-california): Step-by-step ADU permit process — types, requirements, timeline, costs, and approval steps
- [What Is Title 24 Energy Compliance?](https://www.cadtri.com/resources/title-24-energy-compliance-california): Title 24 requirements, CF1R forms, HERS reports, prescriptive vs. performance methods
- [How to Respond to Plan Check Corrections](https://www.cadtri.com/resources/how-to-respond-to-plan-check-corrections): Responding to plan check correction letters — revised drawings, response letters, resubmittal process
- [Garage Conversion ADU in Florida, Texas, and North Carolina](https://www.cadtri.com/resources/garage-conversion-adu-california): Garage conversion permit process, costs, zoning rules, and common approval issues
- [What Is a Pre-Application Meeting?](https://www.cadtri.com/resources/pre-application-meeting-construction): Pre-application meeting process, required documents, and how to prepare

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What is a permit set?**
A permit set is a complete package of architectural drawings and supporting documents submitted to a building department for permit approval. It includes a site plan, floor plans, exterior elevations, building sections, construction details, and energy compliance documentation. Florida uses the Florida Energy Code; Texas cities use IECC-based energy requirements; North Carolina uses the NC Energy Conservation Code.

**What building codes apply in Florida, Texas, and North Carolina?**
Florida uses the Florida Building Code (FBC), which includes strict wind resistance requirements in South Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone. Texas cities adopt the International Residential Code (IRC) or International Building Code (IBC) with local amendments — there is no mandatory statewide residential code. North Carolina uses the North Carolina State Building Code, based on the IBC and IRC with state-specific amendments.

**How long does it take to get a permit in Florida, Texas, and North Carolina?**
Review timelines vary by jurisdiction and project complexity. Florida residential permits typically take 2–6 weeks in major counties. Texas cities range from a few days for simple projects to several weeks for complex work. North Carolina residential permits are typically processed within 1–3 weeks for straightforward projects. Complete, code-compliant submissions consistently move faster through plan check.

**What is plan check in Florida, Texas, and North Carolina?**
Plan check is the review process where a building department examines submitted permit drawings for compliance with building codes, zoning requirements, and local development standards. It may be performed over-the-counter, through an online portal, or by a third-party plan check firm depending on the jurisdiction.

**Do I need a permit for a garage conversion in Florida, Texas, and North Carolina?**
Yes. Converting a garage to habitable space requires a building permit in all three states. The permit set must address structural requirements, insulation, egress, ventilation, fire separation, and energy compliance. Florida projects in Miami-Dade must also satisfy High Velocity Hurricane Zone wind requirements for any new openings.

**What are the types of ADUs in Florida, Texas, and North Carolina?**
ADU rules vary by jurisdiction. Common types include: attached ADUs (connected to the primary residence), detached ADUs (standalone backyard structures), garage conversion ADUs (existing garage converted to habitable space), and interior conversion ADUs (created within the existing home envelope). Florida, Texas, and North Carolina each set ADU rules locally — there is no statewide ADU reform law in these states equivalent to California's.

**What does CADTRI include in an ADU permit package?**
A CADTRI ADU permit package includes a site plan with zoning compliance verification, floor plans showing room layouts and dimensions, exterior elevations, structural coordination, energy compliance documentation appropriate to the jurisdiction, and a complete organized submission formatted for the applicable Florida, Texas, or North Carolina building department.

**What areas does CADTRI serve?**
CADTRI serves clients throughout Florida, Texas, and North Carolina. Florida cities include Miami, Fort Lauderdale, Tampa, Orlando, and Jacksonville. Texas cities include Austin, Dallas, Houston, and San Antonio. North Carolina cities include Charlotte, Raleigh, Durham, and Asheville. All work is performed remotely — clients submit project information online and receive permit-ready drawings digitally.
